channel.title: Salmon on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Salmon on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Salmon Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 15-1/2-oz tin canned salmon
  • 1 cup liquid (liquid from salmon plus milk)
  • ½ cup soft bread crumbs
  • 1 tsp grated onions
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
  • 1 tbsp chopped parsley
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten
  • ½ tsp garlic salt

Instructions

Flake salmon and add salmon liquid and milk. Stir in remaining ingredients and mix well.

Pour into a greased 8-inch pie pan and bake in a 350 degree oven for 45 – 50 minutes or until center is solid when tested with a knife.

item.title: Salmon Bisque
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 Tbsp butter
  • 3-1/2 cups milk
  • 1 onion, finely chopped
  • 7-1/2 oz tin salmon of 1 qt canned lake trout
  • 1 stalk celery, chopped
  • salt and pepper
  • 3 Tbsp flour

Instructions

Melt butter in a saucepan and sauté onion and celery. Add flour and stir well. Add milk slowly, stirring constantly. Add salmon and season with salt and pepper. Cook over low heat until creamy and smooth.

Reheat gently if served a t later date. Serves 4. Makes a hearty first course or a full meal when served with fresh biscuits and salad.

item.title: Fake Salmon
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 7 lbs chopped pike
  • 1 cup vegetable oil
  • 1 cup vinegar
  • 1 cup tomato soup
  • 1-1/2 tbsp salt
  • 1 tbsp pepper garlic powder – optional
  • Parsley – optional

Instructions

Mix last seven ingredients together. Pour over chopped fish and leave in refrigerator overnight.

In the morning, bake at 250 degrees for 5 hours. Pack in sterilized jars.

item.title: Salmon Cheese Ball
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pkg (8 oz) cream cheese, softened
  • 1 tin (7-1/2 oz) salmon, drained & flaked
  • 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • Snipped parsley
  • Snack crackers

Instructions

Mix cream cheese, salmon, Worcestershire sauce, salt and pepper until well blended, about 1 minute. Shape into a ball. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour.

Sprinkle with parsley. Garnish with sliced olives if desired. Serve with crackers.

Option:- Substitute 1 tin (6-1/2 oz) tuna, drained & flaked, for the salmon.

item.title: Salmon Pate
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 (16 oz ) tin red salmon
  • ¼ or ½ lb butter
  • ¼ tsp dill weed (or 1 tsp fresh)
  • 2 Tbsp Miracle Whip
  • Salt & pepper to taste

Instructions

Blend for a few minutes, then chill.
